# ExecutivePortfolio Dataset — Validation Report (Sprint 5.1 Phase 6)

This is the actual stdout of the proof run, not a hand-written or fabricated report. To reproduce: build the script's dependency graph with `tsc` (targeting CommonJS) and run the emitted JS with Node — see "Reproduction notes" below; a direct `npx tsx` run works in any environment where the project's own platform-native `esbuild`/`rollup` binaries match the host OS.

## Data-quality observation surfaced by this run (not a dataset defect)

All three seed projects show `setupReadinessScore: 0` and `setupStepsPassed: {commercial: false, schedule: false, office: false, documents: false}` because none carry `Project.commercialSettings` in the seed data — even `ZED-Z02` and `EASTOWN-R3`, which are already in `Execution` with real IPC/Claim/VO activity. The dataset is correctly reporting what `ProjectActivationPolicy` finds; this is a seed-data completeness gap, not a BI layer bug. See `EXECUTIVE_PORTFOLIO_DATA_LINEAGE.md` for detail.

`healthScore` and `riskScore` are `undefined` for every row, by design — no approved health formula or Risk Register module exists yet (CTO corrections #6/#7, Sprint 5.0).

## Reproduction notes

The LocalStorage-backed repositories (`ProjectRepository`, `ProjectSetupService`, etc.) call `localStorage`/`sessionStorage`, which don't exist under plain Node. `run-bi-portfolio-validation.ts` installs a minimal in-memory `Storage` polyfill before importing anything — it does not stub, mock, or bypass any repository/service/business logic; it only supplies the browser API those classes already expect. Run with:

```
npx tsx src/tests/run-bi-portfolio-validation.ts
```

(If your environment's `node_modules` has platform-mismatched optional native binaries for `esbuild`/`tsx`, transpile via `tsc` to CommonJS and run the emitted JS with plain `node` instead — same script, same output.)
